title: first-run-assets.v1 — the first-run removed-asset manifest
description: The committed list of the first-run-only assets the instantiator removes at the Retire step once setup is sound, at .engine/provisioning/first-run-assets.json. It mirrors instantiator.py's _FIRST_RUN_ASSET_FILES / _FIRST_RUN_ASSET_DIRS (a parity test binds them) so the first-run reference-closure check can read the removed set WITHOUT importing the instantiator it is about to verify nothing references. It is permanent provisioning data — it stays after retirement — and carries two load-bearing lists: `files` (repo-relative paths of the removed files) and `directories` (repo-relative paths of the removed directories), plus an optional plain-language `description`. This schema governs its shape only; the reference-closure check governs its presence and its readability.

description.description: Optional. A plain-language note recording what the manifest is and the parity binding it must keep.

files.description: The repo-relative paths of the first-run files the instantiator removes at retirement. Each a non-empty string; no duplicates.

directories.description: The repo-relative paths of the first-run directories the instantiator removes at retirement. Each a non-empty string; no duplicates. May be empty.
